title: bromus secalinus subsp. commutatus
intro: Bromus secalinus subsp. commutatus, also known as wild barley or cheat grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Bromus secalinus subsp. commutatus include the potential to aid in digestion, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal treatment.
